subject: [PATCH 1/5] iova: switch to augmented rbtree for log(n) allocation

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch switches the IOVA allocator to use an augmented rbtree that tracks free gaps between nodes and their subtrees, allowing for log(n) allocation time instead of O(n). The new data structure maintains two additional fields per node: gap_to_prev (the free gap between this node and its predecessor) and __subtree_max_gap (the maximum gap in the subtree rooted at this node). A new search function walks right-first to prefer high addresses, pruning subtrees with smaller gaps. This change aims to address performance issues under heavy fragmentation, where the original O(n) allocation time could take 20+ seconds and trigger softlockups.
